blocks.content: The gym smelled like streamers and someone's too-strong cologne and the particular energy of a hundred and fifty teenagers who had all decided to pretend this was more glamorous than it was. The lights were low and the DJ was setting up in the corner and the basketball hoops had been taped with orange ribbon.
Eli surveyed it with the expression of a man who had arrived somewhere and found it satisfactory.
Marcus had his arm around Savannah. Eli immediately spotted two people he wanted to talk to and excused himself into the crowd. That left Kit and Daisy at the edge of the gym.
He'd seen her at the door when they all arrived together, and she'd been wearing the dark green dress, and his mom had been right.
That was the whole of it, which was not the whole of it at all, but it was what he had.

blocks.speaker: Kit
blocks.content: Eli goes where the people are.
She led him into the crowd and he followed and the music started and the night began.
